{{神社 |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:長由介神社]] == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}} in {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the {{ill|Kanshō era|ja|寛正}} period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of {{ill|Kanshō|ja|寛正}} (1464). From the second year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq-Nagayuke Shrine-qq]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of {{ill|Kanshō|ja|寛正}} (1464). From the second year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq-Nagayuke Shrine-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:長由介神社]] == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}} in {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the {{ill|Kanshō era|ja|寛正}} period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of {{ill|Kanshō|ja|寛正}} (1464). From the second year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == 外部リンク == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:長由介神社]] == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}} in {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the {{ill|Kanshō era|ja|寛正}} period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of {{ill|Kanshō|ja|寛正}} (1464). From the second year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== 脚注 ==" to "== References =="
revision.text:
{{神社 |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:長由介神社]] == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}} in {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the {{ill|Kanshō era|ja|寛正}} period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of {{ill|Kanshō|ja|寛正}} (1464). From the second year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:長由介神社]] == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}} in {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the {{ill|Kanshō era|ja|寛正}} period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of {{ill|Kanshō|ja|寛正}} (1464). From the second year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:長由介神社]] == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}} in {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the {{ill|Kanshō era|ja|寛正}} period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of {{ill|Kanshō|ja|寛正}} (1464). From the second year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|江戸幕府}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:長由介神社]] == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}} in {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the {{ill|Kanshō era|ja|寛正}} period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of {{ill|Kanshō|ja|寛正}} (1464). From the second year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{神社 |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and Kawashima Shrine. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the {{ill|Kanshō era|ja|寛正}} period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of {{ill|Kanshō|ja|寛正}} (1464). From the second year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]{{125 Shrines of Ise}}

{{神社 |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} (Inner Shrine) within the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}} complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. [[Category:Subordinate shrines of Ise Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[ja:長由介神社]] == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}} in {{ill|Taiki, Mie|ja|大紀町}}, {{ill|Watarai District, Mie|ja|度会郡}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}.Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|ja|瀧原宮}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a {{ill|bunrei|ja|分霊}} (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the {{ill|Kanshō era|ja|寛正}} period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代}} that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of {{ill|Kanshō|ja|寛正}} (1464). From the second year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}}

In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of {{ill|Kenkyū|ja|建久}} (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of {{ill|Kanshō|ja|寛正}} (1464). From the second year of {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Daiki)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20年12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4年1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62年3月31日、1133p. * [[学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013年7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011年1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[ja:長由介神社]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:大紀町の歴史]] [[Category:三重県の神社]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]][[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:category refresh]] [[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]][[Category:qq]] {{125 Shrines of Ise}} [[Category:New]]
