{{神社 |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-Miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-Miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine Ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-Miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-Miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine Ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-Miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō era]] period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-Miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] (1464). From the second year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[:en:紀勢本線|紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[:en:尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. revision.comment: Text replacement - "町の歴史" to "の Municipal History"
revision.text:
{{神社 |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-Miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-Miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine Ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-Miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-Miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine Ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-Miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō era]] period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-Miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] (1464). From the second year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[:en:紀勢本線|紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[:en:尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62nd year 3月31日、1133p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011th year 1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|長由介神社|version=102057340|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taiki,_Mie]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] }} [[ja:長由介神社]] [[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:大紀の Municipal History]]

ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62nd year 3月31日、1133p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011th year 1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|長由介神社|version=102057340|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taiki,_Mie]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] }} [[ja:長由介神社]] [[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:大紀町の建築物]] into [[Category:Buildings in Oki Town]] via bot
revision.text:
{{神社 |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-Miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-Miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine Ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-Miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-Miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine Ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-Miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō era]] period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-Miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] (1464). From the second year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[:en:紀勢本線|紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[:en:尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62nd year 3月31日、1133p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011th year 1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|長由介神社|version=102057340|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taiki,_Mie]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] }} [[ja:長由介神社]] [[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Oki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki]]

Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[:en:紀勢本線|紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[:en:尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62nd year 3月31日、1133p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011th year 1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|長由介神社|version=102057340|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taiki,_Mie]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] }} [[ja:長由介神社]] [[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Oki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28693019]] Wikidata name: Nagayuke-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg|270px]]
鳥居と社殿 |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine. The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686. == Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17. The shrine is situated to the right (east side) of the main hall of Takihara-no-Miya.Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73. The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-Miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}. == Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine Ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-Miya. It faces west. The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]. According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居}}.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687. It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed. The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami. This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-Miya. ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688. It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688. == Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine Ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-Miya. The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami. According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified. After the [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō era]] period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-Miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted. == History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] (1464). From the second year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}}. Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added. At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine. The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43). == 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[:en:紀勢本線|紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[:en:尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある。 == References == {{Reflist|2}} == Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62nd year 3月31日、1133p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011th year 1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}} == External Links == {{ external media | align = | width = 230px | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image1 = [http://www.sengukan.jp/wp-content/themes/sengukan/images/shrine/area01/area01_05.jpg 長由介神社・川島神社] }} * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m13/1305.html 長由介神社（ながゆけじんじゃ）] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会（川島神社の解説を含む） {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|長由介神社|version=102057340|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Taiki,_Mie]] [[Category:Shinto_shrines_in_Mie_Prefecture]] }} [[ja:長由介神社]] [[sn:Nagayuke Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Oki Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Taki]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28693019]] Wikidata name: Nagayuke-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} |prefecture= {{ill|Taiki|qid=Q1203189}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.366278|N|136.426389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |part_of= {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|qid=Q11566292}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine.
The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686.</ref>
== Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17.</ref> The shrine is situated to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main hall of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73.</ref> The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-Miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine Ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It faces west.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居}}.<ref name="om7">Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687.</ref> It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed.<ref name="om7"/>
The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.<ref>Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688.</ref> It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.<ref>Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688.</ref>
== Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine Ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="uy"/> The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami.<ref name="ic"/> According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified.<ref name="om7"/>
After the [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō era]] period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/> ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-Miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted.<ref name="om7"/>
== History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] (1464).
From the second year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮}}.<ref name="om7"/> Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added.<ref name="om7"/> At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43).<ref name="uy"/>
== 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[:en:紀勢本線|紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）<ref>伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ</ref>。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある<ref>主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ</ref>。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref>。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[:en:尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）<ref name="mkr"/>。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある<ref name="mkr"/>。
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62nd year 3月31日、1133p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011th year 1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28693019]] Wikidata name: Nagayuke-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} |prefecture= {{ill|Taiki|qid=Q1203189}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.366278|N|136.426389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |part_of= {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|qid=Q11566292}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine.
The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686.</ref>
== Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17.</ref> The shrine is situated to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main hall of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73.</ref> The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-Miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine Ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It faces west.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}.<ref name="om7">Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687.</ref> It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed.<ref name="om7"/>
The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.<ref>Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688.</ref> It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.<ref>Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688.</ref>
== Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine Ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="uy"/> The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami.<ref name="ic"/> According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified.<ref name="om7"/>
After the [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō era]] period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/> ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-Miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted.<ref name="om7"/>
== History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] (1464).
From the second year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}}.<ref name="om7"/> Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added.<ref name="om7"/> At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43).<ref name="uy"/>
== 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[:en:紀勢本線|紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）<ref>伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ</ref>。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある<ref>主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ</ref>。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref>。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[:en:尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）<ref name="mkr"/>。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある<ref name="mkr"/>。

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q28693019]] Wikidata name: Nagayuke-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |name= 長由介神社・川島神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Nagayuke-jinja.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[度会郡]][[大紀町]]滝原872 |位置= {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|display=inline,title}} |deity= 長由介神・川島神 |社格= {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}所管社 |創建= 不明 |本殿= [[神明造]] |別名= |札所等= |festival= 神嘗祭 |神事= |地図={{location map|Japan Mie|label=長由介神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E|}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200|coord={{coord|34|21|58.6|N|136|25|35|E}}}} |prefecture= {{ill|Taiki|qid=Q1203189}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.366278|N|136.426389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |part_of= {{ill|Takihara-no-miya|qid=Q11566292}} }} '''Nagayuke Shrine''' (長由介神社) is a subordinate shrine (所管社) of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, a detached shrine (別宮) of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Inner Shrine) within the [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]] complex. This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine.
The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686.</ref>
== Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17.</ref> The shrine is situated to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main hall of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73.</ref> The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-Miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine Ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It faces west.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}.<ref name="om7">Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687.</ref> It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed.<ref name="om7"/>
The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.<ref>Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688.</ref> It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.<ref>Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688.</ref>
== Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine Ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="uy"/> The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami.<ref name="ic"/> According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified.<ref name="om7"/>
After the [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō era]] period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/> ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-Miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted.<ref name="om7"/>
== History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] (1464).
From the second year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}}.<ref name="om7"/> Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added.<ref name="om7"/> At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43).<ref name="uy"/>
== 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[:en:紀勢本線|紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）<ref>伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ</ref>。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある<ref>主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ</ref>。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref>。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[:en:尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）<ref name="mkr"/>。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある<ref name="mkr"/>。
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62nd year 3月31日、1133p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011th year 1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

This article also covers the '''Kawashima Shrine''' (川島神社), which is co-located with Nagayuke Shrine.
The subordinate shrines of Takihara-no-Miya include this shrine, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and Kawashima Shrine. All three shrines are located within the grounds of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="om6">Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987), p.686.</ref>
== Overview == Located within the sacred precincts of {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}} in [[:en:Taiki, Mie|Taiki, Mie]], [[:en:Watarai District, Mie|Watarai District, Mie]], [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]].<ref name="uy">Compiled by Ujiyamada City Office (1929): p. 17.</ref> The shrine is situated to the right (east side<ref name="om6"/>) of the main hall of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka Publishing (2008): p. 86.</ref><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p. 73.</ref> The customary order of worship within the Takihara-no-Miya precinct includes {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Takihara Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}}, and then {{ill|Nagayuke Shrine|ja|長由介神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|ja|長由介神社#川島神社|lt=Kawashima Shrine|lt_ja=川島神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagayuke Shrine#Kawashima Shrine|qq=}}.<ref name="gk"/>
== Nagayuke Shrine == Nagayuke Shrine Ranks second among the three auxiliary shrines under the jurisdiction of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> It faces west.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> The pronunciation "nagayuke" is associated with "long life," attracting many worshippers praying for longevity during the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]].<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> According to the ''Jingū Yōkō,'' the shrine building is constructed in the [[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shinmei-zukuri]] style with a plank roof (*itabuki*), surrounded by a single-layered sacred fence (*tamagaki*) with a gate, and features one {{ill|Shinmei torii|ja|神明鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei torii|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鳥居|1=torii|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q234731}}.<ref name="om7">Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 687.</ref> It is noted that during the Edo period, only the shrine building existed.<ref name="om7"/>
The enshrined deity is Nagayuke-no-Kami.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> This deity is regarded as the god overseeing the food offerings (*miké*) of Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/> ''Ōmiya Town History'' suggests a possible connection between Nagayuke and {{ill|Toyouke Ōmikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, given the phonetic similarity to {{ill|Toyouke Daijingu|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyouke Daijingu|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Daijingu|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū), although the enshrined deity remains officially unidentified.<ref>Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): pp. 687–688.</ref> It also posits the possibility that Nagayuke-no-Kami is a [[:en:Bunrei|bunrei]] (divine spirit) of Toyouke Ōmikami.<ref>Compiled by Ōmiya Town History Compilation Committee (1987): p. 688.</ref>
== Kawashima Shrine == Kawashima Shrine Ranks third among the three auxiliary shrines under Takihara-no-Miya.<ref name="uy"/> The original shrine building no longer exists, and the deity is now enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="uy"/> The enshrined deity is Kawashima-no-Kami.<ref name="ic"/> According to ''Ōmiya Town History,'' the enshrined deity remains unidentified.<ref name="om7"/>
After the [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō era]] period, the exact location of Kawashima Shrine was lost. During the Edo period, when there was a widespread revival of auxiliary shrines, Kawashima Shrine was not restored due to the uncertainty of its location. It was only during the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]] that it was revived and enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/> ''Daijingū Gishiki-kai'' speculates that the shrine might have originally been located near the Takigawa River southwest of Takihara-no-Miya, and an old map from the {{ill|Jingū Library|ja|神宮文庫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Library|qq=}} marks "Kawashima Shrine" near the sacred storehouses, although no shrine building is depicted.<ref name="om7"/>
== History == The founding dates of both shrines are unknown. In the ''Annual Events of the Imperial Grand Shrine'' (*皇太神宮年中行事*) from the third year of [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkyū]] (1192), it is noted under the entry for the 23rd day of the sixth lunar month that after visiting the {{ill|Takihara-no-Miya|ja|瀧原宮|lt_ja=瀧原竝宮|lt=Narabi-no-Miya|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takihara-no-Miya|qq=}}, the envoy proceeded to Kawashima, Nagayuke, and Amawakamiya Shrines. However, this entry was added later by Arakida Moritsune in the fifth year of [[:en:Kanshō|Kanshō]] (1464).
From the second year of [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]] (1662), both {{ill|Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|ja|若宮神社 (大紀町)|lt=Wakamiya Shrine|lt_ja=若宮神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Shrine (Taiki, Mie)|qq=}} and Nagayuke Shrine became part of the {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|ja|神宮式年遷宮|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|1=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Shikinen Sengū|qq=}} system managed by the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|ja|Tokugawa shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate|2=ja|3=江戸幕府|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokugawa shogunate|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name="om7"/> Since 1889 (Meiji 22), the reconstruction of the shrines has been overseen by the {{ill|Jingū Administration Office|ja|神宮司庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingū Administration Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 伊勢神宮|1=Ise Grand Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q687168}}.<ref name="om7"/> Until the Edo period, Nagayuke Shrine faced south like Wakamiya Shrine, but in 1874 (Meiji 7), its orientation was changed to the west, and a sacred fence with a gate was added.<ref name="om7"/> At the same time, Kawashima Shrine was formally enshrined together with Nagayuke Shrine.<ref name="om7"/>
The most recent reconstructions of Nagayuke Shrine and Kawashima Shrine were completed in November 2014 (Heisei 26). The previous relocation (*senzasai*) occurred in May 1910 (Meiji 43).<ref name="uy"/>
== 交通 == * [[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[:en:紀勢本線|紀勢本線]][[滝原駅]]より徒歩約36分（約1.9[[キロメートル|km]]）<ref>伊勢文化舎 編（2008）：83ページ</ref>。[[列車]]の本数が少なく、最大2 - 3時間列車が運行されない時間帯があるため、[[旅行ガイドブック]]によっては[[自動車]]利用を勧めるものもある<ref>主婦の友社 編（2011）：44ページ</ref>。 * [[三重交通]][[松阪熊野線]]（旧・南紀特急バス）「瀧原宮前」[[バス停留所|バス停]]下車、すぐ<ref name="mkr">{{Cite web|和書|publisher=三重県観光連盟|url=http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2944.html|title=皇大神宮別宮 瀧原宮|work=観光三重|accessdate=2021-02-06}}</ref>。 * [[紀勢自動車道]][[大宮大台インターチェンジ|大宮大台IC]]から[[:en:尾鷲市|尾鷲]]方面へ南下、[[国道42号]]経由、約10分（約300m）<ref name="mkr"/>。100台分の[[駐車場]]がある<ref name="mkr"/>。
== References == {{Reflist|2}}
== Bibliography == * [[伊勢文化舎]] 編『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、伊勢文化舎、平成20th year 12月23日、151p. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 * 宇治山田市役所 編『宇治山田市史 上巻』宇治山田市役所、昭和4th year 1月20日、862p. * 大宮町史編纂委員会 編『大宮町史 歴史編』[[大宮町 (三重県)|大宮町]]、昭和62nd year 3月31日、1133p. * [[:en:学研パブリッシング|学研パブリッシング]]『伊勢神宮に行こう』Gakken Mook神社紀行セレクションvol.1、[[薗田稔]]監修、学研マーケティング、2013rd year 7月4日、82p. ISBN 978-4-05-610047-1 * [[主婦の友社]] 編『開運!ガールズ♡お伊勢まいり』主婦の友社、2011th year 1月20日、95p. ISBN 978-4-07-276191-5 == See Also == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}
